data.description:
How RND Cook Next JS

Complete Dev Journal & Architecture Guide — rnd-nextjs-template

Audience: Developers  |  Level: Beginner → Intermediate  |  Version: 7

Teacher's note: This guide is self-contained. Everything you need is here — no external doc links. Think of the template as a kitchen: use cases are your knives, services are the prep counter, and AAA in actions is the bouncer at the door.

Table of contents

  1. Introduction & golden rule
  2. Quick start
  3. Where new code goes
  4. FAQ — use case placement, reads vs writes, actions vs hooks, AAA, controllers
  5. Stack & dependencies
  6. Repository layout
  7. Domain layer — request flow
  8. AAA inline template (Authentication, Authorization, Accounting)
  9. Services, use cases, entities
  10. Auth (Better Auth, env, proxy, UI)
  11. Frontend — App Router & Atomic Design
  12. Workflows — add table, UI, page
  13. Full code samples
  14. Cross-cutting concerns
  15. Common mistakes & practice exercises
  16. Part II — Auth deep dive (two layers, sign-in flow, auth() vs getSession)
  17. Part II — Database migrations (generate, migrate, push, troubleshooting)
  18. Part III — Docker MySQL (compose, port 3307, troubleshooting)
  19. Part III — PWA (manifest, service worker, push demo)
  20. Part IV — Install landing (/landing)
  21. Part IV — Design system (light-only UI)
  22. Part IV — Local storage (IndexedDB + Storage API)

1. Introduction & golden rule

Golden rule: Both reads and writes go through actions first (inline AAA), then service → usecase → database.
page / hook  →  action (AAA)  →  service  →  usecase  →  database

No controllers. Pages and hooks import actions. Services are called from actions. Proxy and auth UI use services for session checks only.

2. Quick start

Prerequisites

  • Bun — install: npm install -g bun; upgrade: bun upgrade
  • Docker (recommended for MySQL) or local MySQL 8+

Setup

bun install
cp example.env .env
docker compose up -d          # MySQL on host port 3307
bun run db:migrate
bun run dev

DATABASE_URL defaults to mysql://root:123@127.0.0.1:3307/rnd_template. Docker creates the database automatically.

New sign-ups get role dev. Promote yourself:

UPDATE user SET role = 'admin' WHERE email = 'you@example.com';

Checkpoint ✓ Sign up → sign in → visit /users.

3. Where new code goes

I need to…Put it here
DB tabledatabase/schema.ts
Types + roles + permissionslib/entities/<table>.type.ts
One operationlib/domain/usecases/<table>/<action>.usecase.ts
Compose use caseslib/domain/services/<table>.service.ts
Server action (inline AAA)lib/domain/actions/<table>.actions.ts
auth() for actionslib/domain/services/auth.service.ts
Audit loglib/domain/usecases/auth/log_action.usecase.ts
UI mutation handler<component>.hooks.ts next to component
Push notifications (PWA demo)lib/domain/actions/push.actions.ts
PWA manifestapp/manifest.ts
Service workerpublic/sw.js
Browser storagelib/domain/actions/storage.actions.ts
Install landingapp/landing/page.tsx

4. FAQ

Which table does a use case belong to?

If you write from(this_table), the use case belongs under the slice for this_table. Example: querying user → usecases/users/.

Reads vs writes

TypeEntryActionExample
ReadPageAAA + servicegetUsersAction() in app/users/page.tsx
WriteUI hook / formAAA + servicedeleteUserAction via userCard.hooks.ts

Pages handle { ok: false } → redirect to sign-in. Mutations use redirect() with ?error=.

Exceptions: auth.actions.ts, push.actions.ts, and storage.actions.ts skip AAA. storage.actions.ts is browser-only. proxy.ts guards routes only.

Server actions vs UI hooks

LayerLocation
Server actionlib/domain/actions/<table>.actions.ts
UI hook<component>.hooks.ts — calls server action

Do I need app/<route>/actions.ts?

No. Use lib/domain/actions/<table>.actions.ts.

Do I need a controller?

No. Actions + services only.

5. Stack & dependencies

LayerTechnologyNotes
FrameworkNext.js 16 (16.2.4)App Router; RSC default; Cache Components in use cases
UIReact 19 (19.2.4)Client components with "use client"
LanguageTypeScript 5Path alias @/
StylingTailwind CSS 4PostCSS
AuthBetter Auth ^1.6.23Server-side; Drizzle adapter
DatabaseDrizzle ORM 0.45.2 + mysql2MySQL; schema in database/schema.ts
PWAWeb manifest + service worker + web-pushapp/manifest.ts, public/sw.js, optional push demo

npm scripts (database)

ScriptCommandPurpose
db:generatedrizzle-kit generateGenerate migration SQL
db:migratebun scripts/migrate.mtsRun migrations
db:pushdrizzle-kit pushPush schema (dev only)
db:studiodrizzle-kit studioOpen Drizzle Studio

6. Repository layout

rnd-nextjs-template/
├── app/                 # App Router + manifest.ts
├── components/          # atoms → molecules → organisms (incl. PwaPanel)
├── public/              # icon.svg, sw.js
├── lib/domain/          # actions, services, usecases
├── lib/entities/        # Types, roles, permissions
├── database/            # Drizzle client + schema
├── drizzle/             # Generated migration SQL
├── docker-compose.yml   # MySQL on host port 3307
├── auth.ts              # Better Auth config
├── proxy.ts             # Route protection
└── scripts/migrate.mts

7. Domain layer — request flow

ENTRY:  page.tsx | server form | client hook (handleDelete)
   ↓
ACTION: lib/domain/actions/*.actions.ts  (AAA)
   ↓
SERVICE: lib/domain/services/*.service.ts
   ↓
USECASE: lib/domain/usecases/**
   ↓
DATABASE

8. AAA — inline template

StepCodePurpose
Authenticationawait auth()Session + role; null/expired → error
AuthorizationhasPermission(role, permission)Role must allow action
Accountingawait logAction({...})Audit every outcome

Analogy: Bouncer (auth) → Manager with badge checker (authz) → Receipt printer (accounting).

export async function getUsersAction(): Promise<UserResult<UserSelect[]>> {
  const action = "users:list";
  const permission = USER_PERMISSION.USERS_READ;
 
  try {
    const userSession = await auth();
    if (!userSession || userSession.expired) {
      await logAction({ userId: "anonymous", action, success: false, error: "Authentication required." });
      return { ok: false, error: "Authentication required." };
    }
 
    if (!hasPermission(userSession.user.role, permission)) {
      await logAction({ userId: userSession.user.id, action, success: false, error: "Not authorized.", role: userSession.user.role });
      return { ok: false, error: "You are not authorized for this action." };
    }
 
    const users = await getUsers();
    await logAction({ userId: userSession.user.id, action, success: true, role: userSession.user.role });
    return { ok: true, data: users };
  } catch (error) {
    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unexpected error.";
    await logAction({ userId: "unknown", action, success: false, error: message });
    return { ok: false, error: message };
  }
}

Roles & permissions

RolePermissions
owner, adminusers:read, users:delete
tech, sales, dev, qa, po, pm, financeusers:read only

Default sign-up role: dev. Canonical copy-paste source: lib/domain/actions/users.actions.ts.

Action fileExports
auth.actions.tssignInAction, signUpAction, signOutAction (no AAA)
users.actions.tsgetUsersAction, getDevUsersAction, deleteUserAction
push.actions.tssubscribeUser, unsubscribeUser, sendNotification (PWA demo; no AAA)
storage.actions.tsgetStorageEstimateAction, saveLocalRecordAction, etc. (browser-only; no AAA)

9. Services, use cases, entities

Services

FileUsed byExports
auth.service.tsactions, proxy, auth UIgetSession, getSessionFromHeaders, auth, signIn, signUp, signOut
users.service.tsusers.actions.tsgetUsers, getDevUsers, deleteUser

Use cases

  • One file per operation under lib/domain/usecases/<table>/
  • Reads: "use cache", cacheLife, cacheTag
  • Writes: updateTag("<table>") after mutations
  • Auth use cases call auth.api.* from @/auth

Entities

FileContents
users.type.tsUserSelect, USER_ROLE, USER_PERMISSION, ROLE_PERMISSIONS, hasPermission(), UserResult
auth.type.tsAuthUser, AuthSession, ActionSession, ActionLogEntry, AuthResult

10. Auth

Better Auth (auth.ts)

  • Drizzle adapter → user, session, account, verification tables
  • Email/password enabled
  • nextCookies() plugin for session cookies

Environment variables

VariablePurpose
DATABASE_URLMySQL connection string (e.g. rnd_template)
BETTER_AUTH_SECRETSigning secret
BETTER_AUTH_URLBase URL (e.g. http://localhost:3000)
NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEYPWA push — public VAPID key
VAPID_PRIVATE_KEYPWA push — private VAPID key
VAPID_SUBJECTPWA push — mailto contact

Route protection (proxy.ts)

  • Public: /sign-in, /sign-up, /landing, /api/auth
  • Unauthenticated → redirect /sign-in?callbackURL=...
  • Authenticated on auth pages → redirect /

Auth UI routes

RouteComponentPattern
/sign-inSignInFormServer form → signInAction
/sign-upSignUpFormServer form → signUpAction
/HomesignOutAction when session exists

11. Frontend — App Router & Atomic Design

App routes

PathResponsibility
app/layout.tsxRoot layout, fonts, globals.css
app/page.tsxHome, session, PwaPanel, signOutAction
app/sign-in/page.tsxSuspense → SignInForm
app/users/page.tsxgetUsersAction() → UsersTable
app/landing/page.tsxInstallLanding — public PWA landing

Component layers

components/atoms/Button/         Button.tsx + button.hooks.ts
components/molecules/UserCard/   UserCard.tsx + userCard.hooks.ts
components/molecules/LabelInput/ LabelInput.tsx + labelInput.hooks.ts
components/organisms/UsersTable/ UsersTable.tsx + usersTable.hooks.ts
components/organisms/SignInForm/ sign-in-form.tsx + signInForm.hooks.ts
components/organisms/PwaPanel/     PwaPanel.tsx + pwaPanel.hooks.ts
components/molecules/DesktopWindowFrame/ DesktopWindowFrame.tsx
components/organisms/InstallLanding/ InstallLanding.tsx
components/organisms/StoragePanel/  StoragePanel.tsx

Naming: <camelCaseName>.hooks.ts in same folder — no layer suffix in filename.

Conventions

  • "use client" only when using state/events or UI hooks
  • Pages pass serializable props to organisms
  • UI imports entity types only — never database/ or usecases
  • Forms: LabelInput molecule + field configs in organism hooks

12. Workflows — add a feature

Checklist — new table

  1. database/schema.ts → db:generate + db:migrate
  2. lib/entities/<table>.type.ts
  3. lib/domain/usecases/<table>/ — one file per op
  4. lib/domain/services/<table>.service.ts
  5. lib/domain/actions/<table>.actions.ts — copy AAA from users.actions.ts
  6. app/<route>/page.tsx — call action, handle errors
  7. components/ — atoms → molecules → organisms

Reference slice — user table

LayerPath
Schemadatabase/schema.ts
Entitylib/entities/users.type.ts
Use casesget_users, get_users_by_role, delete_user, get_user_role
Servicelib/domain/services/users.service.ts
Actionslib/domain/actions/users.actions.ts
Pagesapp/users/page.tsx, app/landing/page.tsx
UIUserCard, UsersTable, SignInForm

13. Full code samples

app/users/page.tsx

import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
import { getUsersAction } from "@/lib/domain/actions/users.actions";
import { UsersTable } from "@/components/organisms/UsersTable/UsersTable";
 
export default async function UsersPage({ searchParams }) {
  const params = await searchParams;
  const result = await getUsersAction();
  if (!result.ok) {
    redirect(`/sign-in?callbackURL=/users&error=${encodeURIComponent(result.error)}`);
  }
  return (
    <main>
      <h1>Users</h1>
      <UsersTable users={result.data} redirectTo="/users" error={params.error} />
    </main>
  );
}

components/molecules/UserCard/userCard.hooks.ts

import { deleteUserAction } from "@/lib/domain/actions/users.actions";
 
export const useUserCard = (redirectTo = "/users") => {
  const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
    const formData = new FormData();
    formData.set("id", id);
    formData.set("redirectTo", redirectTo);
    await deleteUserAction(formData);
  };
  return { handleDelete };
};

lib/domain/services/users.service.ts

export async function getUsers() { return getUsersUseCase(); }
export async function getDevUsers() { return getUsersByRole(USER_ROLE.DEV); }
export async function deleteUser(input) { return deleteUserUseCase(input); }

lib/domain/usecases/users/delete_user.usecase.ts

export async function deleteUser(input) {
  await database.delete(user).where(eq(user.id, input.id));
  updateTag("users");
  return { ok: true, data: undefined };
}

lib/entities/users.type.ts (permissions excerpt)

export const USER_PERMISSION = {
  USERS_READ: "users:read",
  USERS_DELETE: "users:delete",
} as const;
 
export function hasPermission(role, permission) {
  return ROLE_PERMISSIONS[role].includes(permission);
}

database/schema.ts (user excerpt)

export const user = mysqlTable("user", {
  id: varchar("id", { length: 36 }).primaryKey(),
  name: varchar("name", { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  email: varchar("email", { length: 255 }).notNull().unique(),
  role: varchar("role", { length: 50 }).notNull().default("dev"),
  // session, account, verification tables also in schema
});

proxy.ts

export async function proxy(request) {
  const session = await getSessionFromHeaders(request.headers);
  const publicPaths = ["/sign-in", "/sign-up", "/landing"];
  if (!session && !publicPaths.includes(pathname)) {
    return redirect to /sign-in?callbackURL=pathname;
  }
  return NextResponse.next();
}

14. Cross-cutting concerns

  • @/ path alias → project root
  • UI depends on entity types only; DB access in use cases only
  • Gated reads + writes enter via actions (AAA)
  • Caching: use cases use cacheTag; mutations call updateTag
  • No client auth SDK — server actions + auth.api in use cases
  • Auth pages wrap organisms in Suspense for searchParams
  • PWA: manifest at app/manifest.ts, service worker at /sw.js
  • Docker MySQL on port 3307 — keep DATABASE_URL in sync
  • Install landing at /landing (public)
  • Light-only UI — DESIGN.MD tokens in globals.css
  • Desktop install landing — wide layout + DesktopWindowFrame preview

15. Common mistakes

  • Server actions in app/<route>/actions.ts — use lib/domain/actions/
  • Pages calling services directly for gated data — use actions with AAA
  • Shared AAA wrapper module — keep inline in each action
  • Importing database/ in components
  • Mixing server action logic into UI hooks

16. Practice exercises

  1. Trace deleteUserAction from button click to SQL — list every file.
  2. Add getUserByIdAction with AAA — which permission?
  3. Why does signInAction skip AAA?
  4. Add a new role with a custom permission — which files change?

Part II — Auth Deep Dive

Teacher's note: Auth confuses everyone at first because there are two layers. Layer 1 keeps strangers off your routes. Layer 2 checks who they are and what they may do inside the app. Both are required — but they do different jobs.

17. Two layers of auth

LayerWhereQuestion it answersUses AAA?
Route guardproxy.tsIs there a valid session cookie?No
Domain authauth() in actionsWho is this user and may they do this?Yes (AuthN + AuthZ)

Layer 1 — proxy.ts (route guard)

Browser request
    ↓
proxy.ts → getSessionFromHeaders()
    ↓
No session + not /sign-in|/sign-up  →  redirect /sign-in?callbackURL=...
Session + on /sign-in|/sign-up       →  redirect /
Else                                 →  allow through

Public paths: /sign-in, /sign-up, /api/auth/*. This layer does not check roles or permissions.

Layer 2 — auth() in actions (AAA)

action called (e.g. getUsersAction)
    ↓
auth() in auth.service.ts
    ├─ getSession()        → Better Auth session (cookie)
    └─ getUserRole(userId) → role column from DB
    ↓
ActionSession { user: { ...session, role }, expired: false }
    ↓
hasPermission(role, USER_PERMISSION.…) → allow or deny

If session exists but role is missing/invalid, auth() returns null — same as unauthenticated for AAA purposes.

18. Sign-in flow (no AAA)

Auth actions intentionally skip AAA — the user is not signed in yet.

SignInForm <form action={signInAction}>
    ↓
signInAction (auth.actions.ts) — parse FormData
    ↓
signIn() service → sign_in.usecase.ts
    ↓
auth.api.signInEmail({ email, password })  ← Better Auth sets cookie
    ↓
redirect(callbackURL) or redirect(/sign-in?error=...)

auth.ts — Better Auth config

export const auth = betterAuth({
  secret: process.env.BETTER_AUTH_SECRET,
  baseURL: process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL,
  emailAndPassword: { enabled: true },
  database: drizzleAdapter(database, { provider: "mysql", schema }),
  plugins: [nextCookies()],  // sets session cookies on server
});

When to use which helper

FunctionUse when
getSession() / getSessionFromHeaders()Auth UI (already signed in?), proxy route guard
auth()Every protected action — need session + DB role for AAA
signIn / signUp / signOutAuth actions only — via auth.service → use cases

19. Auth tables & role column

Better Auth owns these tables in database/schema.ts:

  • user — includes role column (default dev)
  • session — active sessions
  • account — credential provider links
  • verification — email verification tokens

Role is not in the Better Auth session object — it is loaded from DB in get_user_role.usecase.ts when auth() runs.

UPDATE user SET role = 'admin' WHERE email = 'you@example.com';

20. Environment variables (auth)

VariableExamplePurpose
DATABASE_URLmysql://root:123@127.0.0.1:3307/rnd_templateMySQL for Drizzle + Better Auth (Docker default)
BETTER_AUTH_SECRETlong-random-stringCookie/session signing
BETTER_AUTH_URLhttp://localhost:3000Must match browser origin in dev

Checkpoint ✓ If sign-in works but actions fail auth, check role in DB. If cookies fail, check BETTER_AUTH_URL matches your dev URL exactly.

Part II — Database Migrations

Teacher's note: Schema changes are like remodeling the kitchen — plan first, generate a migration script, then apply it. Never edit production DB by hand without a migration file.

21. Migration workflow

  1. Edit database/schema.ts (add table, column, index, etc.)
  2. Run bun run db:generate — Drizzle writes SQL to drizzle/
  3. Review the generated SQL file in drizzle/
  4. Run bun run db:migrate — applies pending migrations
  5. Restart dev server if needed

Commands cheat sheet

CommandWhen to use
bun run db:generateAfter every schema.ts change — creates migration SQL
bun run db:migrateApply migrations (uses scripts/migrate.mts + drizzle/ folder)
npm run db:pushDev shortcut — push schema directly, skips migration files (dev only!)
npm run db:studioVisual browser for tables — great for checking role column after sign-up

scripts/migrate.mts

await migrate(db, { migrationsFolder: "./drizzle" });

Uses DATABASE_URL from .env. Prints clear errors on failure — read the message before retrying.

22. Common migration mistakes

  • Changed schema.ts but forgot db:generate — migrate has nothing new to apply
  • Used db:push in team workflow — teammates miss migration files; use generate + migrate for shared repos
  • Wrong DATABASE_URL — migrating a different database than your app connects to
  • Added role column to existing users table — run migrate; existing rows get default dev
  • Better Auth tables missing — run migrate after initial clone/setup
  • Wrong port in DATABASE_URL — Docker uses 3307, not 3306

23. Part II practice exercises

  1. Sign up a new user, then query SELECT role FROM user WHERE email = '…' — what default do you see?
  2. Promote to admin via SQL, call getUsersAction — can you delete users now?
  3. Add a nullable column to user in schema.ts — generate, review SQL, migrate, verify in db:studio
  4. Trace what happens when an unauthenticated user hits /users — which layer stops them first?

Part III — Docker MySQL & PWA

Teacher's note: Part III covers two practical add-ons: a consistent local database with Docker, and an installable PWA with optional push notifications. Neither changes the AAA flow — they sit beside it.

24. Docker MySQL for local development

The template ships docker-compose.yml so every developer runs the same MySQL without installing it natively — and without fighting port 3306 if you already have MySQL on your machine.

docker compose up -d      # start drizzle-mysql
docker compose down       # stop
bun run db:migrate        # apply schema
SettingDefault
Container namedrizzle-mysql
Host port3307 → container 3306
Databasernd_template (auto-created on first start)
Root password123 (match in compose + .env)
Volumedrizzle_mysql_data — persists data across restarts

DATABASE_URL in .env:

mysql://root:123@127.0.0.1:3307/rnd_template

Troubleshooting

SymptomFix
Unknown database 'rnd_template'Wrong port in .env — use 3307 for Docker, not 3306
Failed to get session after container restartMySQL was down during a request — wait for ready for connections, restart dev server
Schema out of datebun run db:generate then bun run db:migrate
App connects to wrong MySQLLocal MySQL on 3306 vs Docker on 3307 — only one DATABASE_URL wins

Checkpoint ✓ docker ps shows drizzle-mysql → sign up works → /users loads.

25. PWA — Progressive Web App

Installable PWA. Primary install UX at /landing with push + IndexedDB demos.

Files

PathRole
app/manifest.tsWeb app manifest (name, icons, display: standalone)
public/icon.svgApp icon (replace with PNG 192/512 for production)
public/sw.jsService worker — push events + notification click → /
next.config.tsSecurity headers for routes + sw.js (no-cache, CSP)
components/organisms/PwaPanel/Install hints + push subscribe UI
lib/domain/actions/push.actions.tssubscribeUser, unsubscribeUser, sendNotification

Exception: push.actions.ts skips AAA — demo only; subscriptions are in-memory. Production apps should persist subscriptions in the database.

Push setup

  1. Generate VAPID keys: bunx web-push generate-vapid-keys
  2. Add to .env: NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_SUBJECT
  3. Run dev with HTTPS: bunx next dev --experimental-https
  4. Open /landing → Subscribe → Send test notification

Install criteria

  • Valid manifest (app/manifest.ts) — auto-linked by Next.js
  • HTTPS in production; --experimental-https for local push testing
  • iOS: Share → Add to Home Screen (see PwaPanel hints)

Part IV — Landing, Design & Local Storage

Teacher's note: Part IV ties the PWA demos together: a public install landing at /landing, a light-only design system, and browser storage via IndexedDB + the Storage API.

27. Install landing — /landing

The primary PWA install experience lives at /landing — a public route (no sign-in required). Desktop-first layout (max-w-[1360px]): nav header → hero + DesktopWindowFrame preview (xl:grid-cols-2) → horizontal 3-step timeline (lg:grid-cols-3) → PwaPanel + StoragePanel side by side (xl:grid-cols-2).

PathRole
app/landing/page.tsxServer page → InstallLanding
components/organisms/InstallLanding/Desktop hero, horizontal steps, embeds PwaPanel + StoragePanel
components/molecules/DesktopWindowFrame/Window chrome for standalone PWA preview
components/molecules/FuturisticBackdrop/SVG lines + dot grid background
proxy.tsPublic path /landing; auth redirect only on /sign-in, /sign-up

Checkpoint ✓ Open /landing in Chrome with HTTPS → see install CTA when criteria are met.

28. Design system — light-only

  • Fonts: Manrope (display) + Inter (body)
  • Tokens in app/globals.css — teal primary, ochre secondary
  • Tonal surfaces — no 1px borders; use surface-container-*
  • StatusOrb atom for install / persist states
  • DesktopWindowFrame molecule — window chrome on landing preview
  • html { color-scheme: light; }

29. Local storage — IndexedDB + Storage API

Demo on /landing — Storage API quota, persistent mode, IndexedDB records.

storagePanel.hooks → storage.actions → storage.service → usecases/storage → IndexedDB
LayerPath
Typeslib/entities/storage.type.ts
Use caseslib/domain/usecases/storage/
Servicelib/domain/services/storage.service.ts
Actionslib/domain/actions/storage.actions.ts (browser-only; no AAA)
UIcomponents/organisms/StoragePanel/ — quota card, persistent storage, IndexedDB demo

Layout: Full-width header (title + StatusOrb) → quota metrics + progress bar + usage chips → persistent storage + IndexedDB. When embedded inside the landing half-column, bottom sections stack vertically.

30. Part IV practice exercises

  1. Open /landing without signing in — check proxy.ts public paths.
  2. Save a key/value in IndexedDB — trace hook → action → service → use case.
  3. Request persistent storage before and after PWA install.
  4. Compare StoragePanel layout with embedded={true} vs standalone — when do sections stack?

26. Part III practice exercises

  1. Start Docker MySQL, run migrate, sign up — verify user row in db:studio
  2. Stop the container mid-request — what error do you see? Fix by waiting for MySQL + restarting dev
  3. Generate VAPID keys, subscribe to push, send a test notification — trace which files run
  4. Why does push.actions.ts skip AAA but users.actions.ts does not?

You've got this. Copy the AAA template, follow the folder table, ship one small feature at a time. That's how RND cooks Next.js.
